title: taxus calcicola
intro: Taxus calcicola, also known as Himalayan yew, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the yew family (Taxaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily for its medicinal properties derived from the bark and leaves, which contain taxines and taxol. Taxus calcicola is believed to have health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction for herbal use, although it is essential to note that Taxus calcicola is toxic and should be handled with caution.
